volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 2.3.255 - Section 2.5.324b)

sections.label: Section 2.3.255
sections.original: servo frugi atque ubi illi bene sit ligno , aqua calida , cibo ,
sections.translation: an honest servant , where she ’ ll be well off , in wood , warm water , food , and clothing , and where she may properly bring up the children which she may have , in preference to that rascally servant of an armour-bearer [*] , a good-for-nothing and dishonest , a fellow that hasn ’ t this day a leaden dump of money his own .

sections.label: Section 2.3.259
sections.original: Mirum ecastor , te senecta aétate officium tuom
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I am surprised that in your old age you

sections.label: Section 2.3.260
sections.original: non meminisse .
sections.translation: do not remember your duty .

sections.label: Section 2.3.260b
sections.original: Quid iam ?
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.260c
sections.original: Quia , si facias recte aut commode ,
sections.translation: Because if you were to act rightly or becomingly , you ’ d let me manage the maid-servants , which is my own province .

sections.label: Section 2.3.262
sections.original: Qui , malum , homini scutigerulo dáre lubet ?
sections.translation: Why the plague do you wish to give her to a fellow that carries a shield ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.262b
sections.original: Quía enim filio
sections.translation: Because it ’ s our duty to gratify our only son .

sections.label: Section 2.3.263b
sections.original: At quamquam unicust ,
sections.translation: But although he is an only one , not a bit the more is he my only son than I am his only father .

sections.label: Section 2.3.265
sections.original: illum mi aequiust quam me illi quae volo concedere .
sections.translation: It ’ s more becoming for him to conform to me , than for me to him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.266
sections.original: Tu ecastor tibi , homo , malam rem quaeris .
sections.translation: By my troth , sir , you ’ re providing for yourself a serious piece of trouble .

sections.label: Section 2.3.266b
sections.original: Subolet , sentio .
sections.translation: She suspects it , I find that . ( To his wife .) What , I , do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.267b
sections.original: Tu . nam quid friguttis ? quid istuc tam cupide cupis ?
sections.translation: You ; but why do . you stammer so ? Why do you wish for this with such anxiety ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.268
sections.original: Vt enim frugi servo detur potius quam servo improbo .
sections.translation: Why , that she may rather be given to a servant that ’ s honest , than to a servant that ’ s dishonest .

sections.label: Section 2.3.269
sections.original: Quid si ego impetro atque exoro a vilico , causa mea
sections.translation: What if I prevail upon , and obtain of the bailiff , that for my sake

sections.label: Section 2.3.270
sections.original: ut eam illi permittat ?
sections.translation: he ’ ll give her up to the other one ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.270b
sections.original: Quid si ego autem ab armigero impetro ,
sections.translation: But what if I prevail upon the armour-bearer to give her up to the other one ? And I think that I can prevail upon him in this .

sections.label: Section 2.3.272
sections.original: Convenit . vin tuis Chalinum huc evocem verbis foras ?
sections.translation: That ’ s agreed upon . Should you like that , in your name , I should call Chalinus hither out of doors ? Do you beg of him , and I ’ ll beg of the bailiff .

sections.label: Section 2.3.273b
sections.original: Sane volo .
sections.translation: I ’ m quite willing .

sections.label: Section 2.3.274
sections.original: Iam hic erit . nunc experiemur , nostrum uter sit blandior .—
sections.translation: He ’ ll be here just now . Now we ’ ll make trial which of us two is the most persuasive . ( She goes into the house .)

sections.label: Section 2.3.275
sections.original: Hercules dique istam perdant , quod nunc liceat dicere .
sections.translation: May Hercules and the Gods confound her !— a thing that now I ’ m at liberty to say . I ’ m wretchedly distracted with love ; but she , as though on purpose , thwarts me . My wife has some suspicion now of this that I ’ m planning ; for that reason is she purposely lending her assistance to the armour-bearer .

sections.label: Section 2.4.279
sections.original: qui illum di omnes deaeque perdant .
sections.translation: May all the Gods and Goddesses confound him !

sections.label: Section 2.4.279b
sections.original: Te uxor aiebat tua
sections.translation: Your wife said that you were calling me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.280b
sections.original: Ego énim vocari iussi .
sections.translation: Why yes , I did order you to be sent for .

sections.label: Section 2.4.280c
sections.original: Eloquere quid velis .
sections.translation: Tell me what you want .

sections.label: Section 2.4.281
sections.original: Primum ego te porrectiore fronte volo mecum loqui ;
sections.translation: In the first place , I want you to speak to me with a more cheerful countenance .

sections.label: Section 2.4.282
sections.original: stultitia est ei te esse tristem , cuius potestas plus potest .
sections.translation: It would be folly for me to be morose toward you whose rule is the strongest .

sections.label: Section 2.4.283
sections.original: probum te et frugi hominem iam pridem esse arbitror .
sections.translation: Indeed ! I consider you to be an honest fellow .

sections.label: Section 2.4.283b
sections.original: Intellego .
sections.translation: So I find .

sections.label: Section 2.4.284_285
sections.original: quin , si ita arbitrare , emittis me manu ?
sections.translation: But if you think so , why don ’ t you give me my freedom ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.287
sections.original: Quid velis modo id velim me scire .
sections.translation: What you wish , I only wish myself to be acquainted with it .

sections.label: Section 2.4.287b
sections.original: Ausculta , ego eloquar .
sections.translation: Listen then ; I ’ ll tell you . I ’ ve promised to give Casina as a wife to our bailiff .

sections.label: Section 2.4.289
sections.original: At tua uxor filiusque promiserunt mihi .
sections.translation: But your wife and your son have promised her to me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.289b
sections.original: Scio .
sections.translation: I know it ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.290
sections.original: sed utrum nunc tu caelibem te ésse mavis liberum
sections.translation: but whether now would you prefer yourself to be single and a free man , or , as a married man , to pass your lives , yourself and your children , in slavery ? This choice is your own : whichever condition of these two you prefer , take it .

sections.label: Section 2.4.293
sections.original: Liber si sim , meo periclo vivam ; nunc vivo tuo .
sections.translation: If I am free , I live at my own cost ; at present I live at yours . As to Casina , I ’ m resolved to give way to no born man .

sections.label: Section 2.4.295
sections.original: Intro abi atque actutum uxorem huc evoca ante aedis cito ,
sections.translation: Go in-doors , and at once be quick and call my wife here , out of doors ; and bring hither together with you an urn [*] , with some water , and the lots .

sections.label: Section 2.4.296b
sections.original: Satis placet .
sections.translation: I ’ m quite agreable .

sections.label: Section 2.4.297
sections.original: Ego pol istam iam aliquovorsum tragulam decidero .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , in some way or other I ’ ll now ward off this weapon of yours ; for if , as it is , I shall not be able to prevail by persuasion , at least I ’ ll try it by lot . There I shall take vengeance upon you and your abettors .

sections.label: Section 2.4.299b
sections.original: Attamen
sections.translation: Still , for all that the lot will fall to me —

sections.label: Section 2.4.300b
sections.original: Vt quidem pol pereas cruciatu malo .
sections.translation: Aye , faith , for you to go to perdition with direful torments .

sections.label: Section 2.4.301
sections.original: Mi illa nubet , machinare quid lubet quo vis modo .
sections.translation: She shall marry me , contrive what you will , in any way you please .

sections.label: Section 2.4.302
sections.original: Abin hinc ab oculis ?
sections.translation: Won ’ t you away hence from my sight ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.302b
sections.original: Invitus me vides , vivam tamen .—
sections.translation: Unwillingly you look upon me , still I shall live on . ( Goes into the house .)

sections.label: Section 2.4.303
sections.original: Sumne ego miser homo ? satin omnes res sunt advorsae mihi ?
sections.translation: Am I not a wretched man ? Don ’ t all things go quite contrary with me ? I ’ m now afraid that my wife will prevail upon Olympio not to

sections.label: Section 2.4.305
sections.original: ne Casinam ducat : si id factum est , ecce me nullum senem ;
sections.translation: marry Casina . If that ’ s done , why look , it ’ s all over with me in my old age ! If she does not prevail , there is still some tiny hope in the lots . But if the lots fail me , I ’ ll make a pillow of a sword , and lay me down upon it . But see , most opportunely Olympio ’ s coming out of doors .

sections.label: Section 2.5.309
sections.original: Vna edepol opera in furnum calidum condito
sections.translation: By my faith , all in an instant shut me up in a hot furnace ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.310
sections.original: atque ibi torreto me pro pane rubido ,
sections.translation: and parch me there for a hard-baked biscuit [*] , good mistress , before you shall gain that point of me which you desire .

sections.label: Section 2.5.312
sections.original: Salvos sum , salva spes est , ut verba audio .
sections.translation: I ’ m all right . My hope ’ s realized , according as I hear his words .

sections.label: Section 2.5.313
sections.original: Quid tu me tua , era , libertate territas ?
sections.translation: But why do you frighten me about liberty ? Why , even though you should oppose it , and your son as well ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.315
sections.original: vobis invitis atque amborum ingratiis
sections.translation: against your wills and in spite of you both , for a single penny [*] I can become free .

sections.label: Section 2.5.317
sections.original: Quid istúc est ? quicum litigas , Olympio ?
sections.translation: What ’ s this ? Who are you wrangling with , Olympio ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.318
sections.original: Cum eadem qua tu semper .
sections.translation: With the same person that you always are .

sections.label: Section 2.5.318b
sections.original: Cum uxoré mea ?
sections.translation: What , with my wife ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.319
sections.original: Quam tu mi uxorem ? quasi venator tu quidem es :
sections.translation: What wife are you speaking of to me ? Really you are a hunter , as it were :

sections.label: Section 2.5.320
sections.original: dies átque noctes cum cane aetatem exigis .
sections.translation: your nights and days you pass with a female cur [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.5.321
sections.original: Quid agit , quid loquitur tecum ?
sections.translation: What does she say ? What ’ s she talking to you about ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.321b
sections.original: Orat , obsecrat ,
sections.translation: She ’ s begging and entreating of me that I won ’ t be taking Casina as my wife .

sections.label: Section 2.5.322b
sections.original: Quid tu postea ?
sections.translation: What did you say after that ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.323
sections.original: Negavi enim ipsi me concessurum Iovi ,
sections.translation: Why , I declared that I wouldn ’ t give way to Jupiter himself , if he were to entreat me .

sections.label: Section 2.5.324b
sections.original: Di te servassint mihi .
sections.translation: May the Gods preserve you for me !
